mechanism_narrative: A persistently low unemployment rate signals a robust labor market and strong consumer confidence, which underpins corporate earnings and overall economic stability. This positive environment generally supports higher equity valuations over the medium term.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative on average when the unemployment rate is below its 12-month moving average.

mechanism_narrative: A significant acceleration in consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) indicates overheating in the economy, which typically prompts the central bank to tighten monetary policy. Higher interest rates increase discount rates for equities, leading to lower valuations.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 40% of instances where CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change exceeds 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Robust growth in retail sales (RSAFS) combined with increasing housing starts (HOUST) signifies strong consumer demand and investment activity. This broad-based economic strength translates into higher corporate revenues and profits, driving equity prices upward.
failure_condition: When both RSAFS and HOUST show strong growth, S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are flat or negative on average over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic contraction, a sustained decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals weakening manufacturing activity and overall supply-side distress. This directly impacts corporate earnings and investor sentiment, leading to negative equity returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 30% of instances where INDPRO declines significantly during a recognized recessionary period.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in real Gross Domestic Product (GDPC1) indicates a healthy and expanding economy. This fundamental strength provides a fertile ground for corporate profitability and investor confidence, supporting positive equity market performance over the longer term.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are negative on average when GDPC1 shows sustained growth above 2.0% annualized over any 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly gains in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflect a robust and expanding labor market. This leads to increased consumer income and spending, which directly boosts corporate revenues and is quickly reflected in positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are negative in more than 50% of instances where PAYEMS growth exceeds 150,000 in a month.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signals a tightening monetary policy stance by the central bank. This raises borrowing costs for businesses and consumers, dampening economic activity and reducing corporate profitability, leading to lower equity valuations.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive on average when FEDFUNDS has increased by more than 0.25% over three months.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of accelerating retail sales (RSAFS) and a declining unemployment rate (UNRATE) indicates strong consumer confidence and purchasing power. This dual strength in demand and labor market health provides a powerful tailwind for corporate earnings, driving positive equity returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative or flat on average when both retail sales are growing and unemployment is falling.
